All of the aboveThe presence of nodal metastasis stage III decreases survival Patents with stages I and II can expect excellent survival which includes those with T4 disease where tumors invade into other organs or tissues without nodal metastases. The overall 5 year survival rate for all SEER stages of colon cancer is 65% and 67% for rectal cancer according to ACS. In colon cancer patient’s age and comorbidity predict survival, in rectal cancer patients neither age or comorbidity significantly impact survival Tumors associated with MSI appear to have different biologic characteristics than tumors that result from the LOH pathway. Tumors with MSI are more likely to be in the right colon and possess diploid DNA and are associated with a better prognosis than tumors that arise from the LOH pathway that are microsatellite stable.
